B+ Tree Index: PostgreSQL uses B+ trees for all standard indexes (CREATE INDEX). Leaf nodes are doubly linked for range scans. InnoDB's clustered index stores actual row data in leaf nodes.
MVCC: PostgreSQL implements MVCC by storing xmin/xmax transaction IDs in each tuple. Old versions are cleaned up by VACUUM. MySQL InnoDB uses undo logs for version chain.
WAL: PostgreSQL's Write-Ahead Log ensures crash recovery — changes are logged before being applied to data pages. pg_wal directory contains WAL segments. Similar to Redis AOF and ext4 journaling.
JOIN: PostgreSQL's query planner chooses between Nested Loop (small tables), Hash Join (equality joins, larger tables), and Merge Join (pre-sorted data). EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows which was chosen.
Normalization: Instagram stores user data in normalized form (users, posts, comments as separate tables) for write efficiency. Analytics warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery) use denormalized star schemas for read performance.
Transaction Isolation: PostgreSQL default is Read Committed. MySQL InnoDB default is Repeatable Read. Banking systems typically use Serializable. Connection poolers (PgBouncer) may affect transaction semantics.
